raw_consumer_statement:
Issue : Credit card dispute services not rendered Company : Citibank Merchant : XXXX Amount : {$320.00} Complaint : I am filing a complaint regarding a {$320.00} charge from XXXX that Citibank has refused to reverse, despite the fact that no service was provided and the merchant can not verify that a valid reservation ever existed. 

I attempted to book a hotel stay for XX/XX/year> in XXXX, Utah through XXXX. At the time of booking, the listing stated that the reservation could be canceled by XX/XX/XXXX with no penalty. However, after completing the transaction, I never received a confirmation number or confirmation email, and I was not provided any booking receipt or contract. 

When I became sick and needed to cancel prior to the stated deadline, I attempted to contact XXXX. They informed me that they could not locate any reservation under my name and were unable to assist me without a confirmation number.

I then contacted the hotel directly. Initially, they stated that no reservation existed under my name. Later, after I had told the hotel I was disputing the charge they claimed they had found a reservation under my name ; however, they were unable to provide a confirmation number, booking ID, or any verifiable documentation of the reservation. They also could not confirm that any check-in or stay occurred. 

I made multiple good-faith attempts to contact XXXX to resolve this issue ; however, their customer support system requires a valid confirmation number to access assistance. Because I was never provided a confirmation number or booking email, I was unable to connect with a live representative or manage the reservation in any way. As a result, I was effectively blocked from verifying, modifying, or canceling the booking, leaving me with no reasonable way to resolve the issue directly with the merchant.

At no point was I able to access, cancel, or use a reservation, and no service was ever provided.

Despite these facts, Citibank denied my dispute and continues to hold me responsible for this charge. The merchant has not provided proof of a valid reservation, confirmation number, or any documentation showing that a service was rendered.

This charge is unverifiable and clearly qualifies as services not rendered.
